steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage establishes a clear pattern: Shang dynasty bronze artifacts contain a rare form of lead — bronze artifacts from after the Shang dynasty do not. The blank must state the most logical inference from that absence.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Shang dynasty bronze pieces are rare and therefore more valuable than those from other time periods.: the passage never claims Shang pieces are more valuable; rarity of the lead type does not imply rarity or higher worth of the artifacts. ✗
  • Choice B — the source of some of the raw materials used to make bronze was exploited only until the end of the Shang dynasty.: if the lead source stopped being used at the end of the Shang dynasty, later artifacts would naturally lack it — this directly matches the pattern. ✓
  • Choice C — bronze was used for a short time during the Shang dynasty before different metals were used to make artifacts.: the passage explicitly mentions “bronze artifacts from China dated after the Shang dynasty,” so bronze continued to be used afterward. ✗
  • Choice D — methods used to analyze bronze artifacts are not useful on pieces that are dated after the Shang dynasty.: the passage never discusses analytical methods; this is entirely unsupported. ✗
That is the correct answer.
